title: TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike
exclusions: Does NOT measure certificate-expiry posture (covered by 22.41.2); focuses on observed handshake protocol/cipher.
evidence: Saved search 'tls_downgrade_spike' (hourly) emits a per-endpoint handshake profile and alerts on legacy-cipher spikes.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 4.2.1 strong cryptography in transmission is directly measured.
Transmission security is required for ePHI.
SC-8 transmission confidentiality; legacy cipher negotiation is a direct failure.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An external load-balancer begins negotiating TLS 1.0 with 5% of clients after a config push; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All handshakes remain TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers; no alert.

dataSources: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results.
spl:
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
description: Monitors observed TLS handshake metadata for any endpoint and alerts when legacy-cipher or legacy-protocol negotiations exceed a small threshold. Downgrade is a common attacker technique against partially-upgraded infrastructure.
value: Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.
implementation: (1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback.
visualization: Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.006 Impair Defenses: Indicator Blocking

knownFalsePositives: Legacy client populations (POS devices) can inflate TLS 1.0 usage in scope of a documented risk-acceptance; allow-list such endpoints.
